newsom's.line: 49 ContributorsTranslationsItalianoDivers LyricsThe seventh and longest song on…
newsom's.lineNext: (And I am his, if I am not deceived)
stanza: 49 ContributorsTranslationsItalianoDivers LyricsThe seventh and longest song on…
(And I am his, if I am not deceived)
Who takes one breath above, for every hour below the sea;
Who gave to me a jewel
Worth twice this woman's life (but would cost her less
Than laying at low tide
To see her true love phosphoresce)

newsom.line: 122 ContributorsTranslationsEspañolItalianoOnly Skin Lyrics“Only Skin” is the l…
newsom.lineNext: That night black airplanes flew over the sea
stanza: 122 ContributorsTranslationsEspañolItalianoOnly Skin Lyrics“Only Skin” is the l…
That night black airplanes flew over the sea
And they were lowing and shifting like
Beached whales
Shelled snails
As you strained and you squinted to see
The retreat of their hairless and blind cavalry

neatly.linePrev: As it cut through the hustling breeze
neatly.line: And I watched how the water was kneading so neatly
stanza: And I watched how the water was kneading so neatly
Gone treacly
Nearly slowed to a stop in this heat;
In a frenzy coiling flush along the muscles beneath

partner.line: Nearly slowed to a stop in this heat;

neatly.linePrev: Gene took off his hat and I noticed his hair
neatly.line: It was neatly trimmed but a patch was bare
neatly.lineNext: I knew it wasn't new wave, it was human error
stanza: He said the power of metal will never be harnessed
I thought the wages of metal should be heavily garnished
We were waitin' for his dad to meet us there
Gene took off his hat and I noticed his hair
It was neatly trimmed but a patch was bare
I knew it wasn't new wave, it was human error

nuisance.linePrev: In tiny nooses, safe from everyone —
nuisance.line: Nothing but a nuisance; gone now, dead and done —
nuisance.lineNext: Be a woman. Be a woman
stanza: I see the blossoms broke and wet after the rain
Little sister, he will be back again
I have washed a thousand spiders down the drain
Spiders’ ghosts hang, soaked and dangling
Silently from all the blooming cherry trees
In tiny nooses, safe from everyone —
Nothing but a nuisance; gone now, dead and done —
Be a woman. Be a woman

nightjar.linePrev: Anecdotes cannot say what Time may do
nightjar.lineNext: When our men are all asleep:
stanza: I kid with Rufous Nightjar
When our men are all asleep:
“It ain’t about how rare you are
But how hard you are to see
Take, you and me—"

nightjar.linePrev: White star, white ship–Nightjar, transmit: transcend!
nightjar.line: White star, white ship–Nightjar, transmit: trans-
stanza: White star, white ship–Nightjar, transmit: transcend!
White star, white ship–Nightjar, transmit: transcend!
White star, white ship–Nightjar, transmit: trans-

nightjar.linePrev: No time. No flock. No chime, no clock. No end
nightjar.line: White star, white ship–Nightjar, transmit: transcend!
nightjar.lineNext: White star, white ship–Nightjar, transmit: transcend!
stanza: A shore, a tide, unmoored–a sight, abroad:
A dawn, unmarked, undone, undarked (a god)
No time. No flock. No chime, no clock. No end
White star, white ship–Nightjar, transmit: transcend!

nacre.line: You do not take her for a sign in nacre on a stone
stanza: A woman is alive!
A woman is alive;
You do not take her for a sign in nacre on a stone
Alone, unfaceted and fine
And never will I wed
I'll hunt the pearl of death to the bottom of my life
And ever hold my breath
Till I may be the diver's wife

novel.line: Ululate the lost Great American Novels —
novel.lineNext: An unlawful lot, left to stutter and freeze, floodlit
stanza: And as for my inflammatory writ?
Well, I wrote it and I was not inflamed one bit
Advice from the master derailed that disaster;
He said "Hand that pen over to me, poetaster!"
While across the great plains
Keening lovely & awful
Ululate the lost Great American Novels —
An unlawful lot, left to stutter and freeze, floodlit
(But at least they didn't run
To their undying credit.)

partner.line: Keening lovely & awful

novel.linePrev: You say I'm like the ice, I freeze
stanza: They say I'm too young to love you
I don't know what I need
They think I don't understand
The freedom land of the seventies
I think I'm too cool to know ya
You say I'm like the ice, I freeze
I'm churning out novels like
Beat poetry on amphetamines

notion.linePrev: It's a means to an end, but the ends don't seem to meet
notion.line: Walking around living the dream anytime I take the notion
notion.lineNext: Till the truth comes bubbling up so bittersweet
stanza: Time and time again, Lord, I been going through the motions
It's a means to an end, but the ends don't seem to meet
Walking around living the dream anytime I take the notion
Till the truth comes bubbling up so bittersweet

partner.line: Time and time again, Lord, I been going through the motions

notion.line: The notion that these lives do never end
notion.lineNext: And all day long we talk about mercy:
stanza: Bury this bone to gnaw on it later;
Gnawing on the telephone, and
'Till then, we pray and suspend
The notion that these lives do never end

nautical.linePrev: But ships are fallible, I say
nautical.line: And the nautical, like all things, fades
nautical.lineNext: And I can recall our caravel:
stanza: We sailed away on a winter's day
With fate as malleable as clay
But ships are fallible, I say
And the nautical, like all things, fades
And I can recall our caravel:
A little wicker beetle shell
With four fine masts and lateen sails
Its bearings on Cair Paravel

stanza: We heard John Lennon play

We thought he hung the moon
The sunlight on your floor

Another country where he'd never been before
You taught him how to do the things the lovers do
Like coming through and loving you
Like coming through and loving you

neutral.linePrev: Oh the click click clack of the heavy black trains
neutral.lineNext: Oh the tick tock tick of the ticking time bomb
stanza: And it rained all night and washed the filth away
Down New York air conditioned drains
Oh the click click clack of the heavy black trains
A million engines in neutral
Oh the tick tock tick of the ticking time bomb
Fifty feet of concrete underground
One little leak becomes a lake
Says the tiny voice in my earpiece
So I give in to the rhythm, the click click clack
I'm too wasted to fight back
Oh tick tock goes the pendulum
On the old grandfather clock
